taxonomy: Iria disticha is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The plant has been classified to belong to the genus Iria, which is part of the order Lamiales. The classification of Iria disticha is further broken down into its binomial nomenclature, consisting of the genus name Iria and the species name disticha. This classification helps scientists identify and study the plant's unique characteristics and properties.
uses: Iria disticha, also known as the African violet, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In addition, Iria disticha has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its anti-diarrheal properties.
